pages.raw_text:
a reception to-. the heads Of De.
partments Of the Spokane post
office and their wives will be given
tonight at the home of Postmaster
Edris. S1303 Walnut street.
Andrew Billed tnsmber Of the
painters union was buried this
afternoon at Greenwood cemetery.
He died Tuesday at his home,
01321 Normandie street. aged 80.
The German Lutheran church
Ladies' Aid society will hold q
fair at Turner hall, rs Third av-
enue, November %.


The orands Ronds valley, In
eastern Oregon, has sent word that
1t will make q large exhibit at the
National Apple show, taking one
section. This Is the prizez apple
district Of eastern Oregon.
Rev. w. !. HindIey left for
Wenatchee last night to deliver
the chief address this evening at
the formation of the new Wenat-
chez $4,000 club. He carried with
him resolution expressing the
best wishes of the Spokane cham
Der of commerce.


The funeral Of Mrs. Grace HurI-
but took place this Afternoon, fol-
lowed by burial at Greenwood
cemetery. Her death occurred last
Tuesday and she is survived by
husband and daughter.


The Spokane club has issued 500
invitations for a dance to be given
at its club room November IL.


A Halloween party will be given
tonight by the Sunday school class
es Of the Vincent M. e. church,
Main avenue and Lincoln.


A reception will be given by the
trustees and ladies of St. Johns
Episcopal church to their pastor,
Rev. e. w. Copper, and wife, next
Thursday evening, November A, at
Sharp avenue.


Managers Of the National Apple
show have In the last few days
had assurances of. exhibits from
Fargo, N. D., Hadenville, Mass,
Peterborough, Ont. Cornell uni
versity and the Bitter Root valley
in Montana. Personal invitations
to friends in the east to attend
the show are being sent out by
members of the 10000 club.


Ladies Of the Ross Park, Twen-
tieth Century club attended a four
course Japanese luncheon. clad In
kimonos, and ate with chop sticks,
seated on the floor in true Japa.
nese fashion, at the home of Mrs.
2. Pfile, Ell Eighth avenue, yes
terday afternoon.


The members Of the Spokane
Grocers' association, their clerks
and their wives, will attend lee
ture on craftsmanship next Tues
day evening at the Y. M. c. A.


A benefit will be given this even
ing at the Central Christian
church, Third and Stevens, for the
old peoples home. A chorus of
SO young women clad in white will
be present. The funds will be
used in the furnishing of a hospital
room.


With the full ceremoniais of a
spiritualist wedding. Fred A. Maud
lin and Lizzie Good were united In
marriage yesterday afternoon at
the rooms of Mrs. Cora Kincannon
Smith In the Nichols block.


The two lodges Of the Eastern
Star will give Halloween social
this evening at the Masonic tenn
pie.


Uneanny spirits = the season
will be present tonight at Hal
lowe'en party to be- given in the
church parlors by the young peo-
pies societies of the First Meth
odist church.


A recital will be given In the
of the South Central
high school November "a by Miss
Flossie ID. Dillon, elocution instruc-
tor at the school.


II. 3. Dewey, state superinten-
dent of schols, will be at the Of
flee of County superintendent
in the court house to-
morning to receive any
calls with reference to the affairs |
of the schools.


The Spokane delegation to the |
Dry Farming congress Is expected |
home this evening. H. l.. Moody |
and Arthur Hooker represented |
the irrigation congress and L. G.
Monroe the chamber of commerce.
